revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天岩戸神社 (福知山市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Amanoiwato Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:天岩戸神社 概観.JPG | location = 206-1 Nichihiruga-take, Butsushōji, Ōe-chō, {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35.4310028|N|135.1504556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Kushi Mikenu no Mikoto | Rank = | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | Annual_Festival = | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}} }} '''Amanoiwato Shrine''' (天岩戸神社, Amanoiwato-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Butsushōji, Ōe-chō, {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture. == Overview == The shrine is one of the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in the Ōe area of the city (details at '''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Shrine|qq=}}'''). Amanoiwato Shrine is considered the inner shrine of Kōtai Jingu Shrine. {|class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō, Uchimiya, Fukuchiyama |Origin of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine) |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō, Amatauchi, Fukuchiyama |Origin of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine) |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō, Butsushōji, Fukuchiyama |Inner shrine of Kōtai Jingu Shrine |} == Enshrined deity == * '''Kushi Mikenu no Mikoto''' Alternatively, the shrine is said to enshrine both {{ill|Kushi Iwamado no Mikoto|ja|櫛岩窓戸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushi Iwamado no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyo Iwamado no Mikoto|ja|豊岩窓戸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo Iwamado no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Grounds == Behind the shrine building is the Gosaishi, a {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|qid=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}}, or sacred rock, which is revered as part of the shrine's grounds. The shrine building is constructed on a rocky base, and visitors must climb using attached chains to reach it. == Practical Information == ; Address * 206-1 Nichihiruga-take, Butsushōji, Ōe-chō, {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Kyoto Tango Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19727758}} {{ill|Miyafuku Line|en|Miyafuku Line|qid=Q8192675|lt=Miyafuku Line|1=Miyafuku Line}} {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|en|Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|qid=Q8081450|lt=Ōeyamaguchi-Nai Shrine Station|1=Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station}} (approximately 20 minutes on foot) ; Nearby [[File:日室ヶ嶽_(大江町).JPG|thumb|right|200px|Iwatoyama ({{ill|Kannabi|en|Kannabi|qid=Q11588874|lt=Kannabi|1=Kannabi}})]] * '''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' * Iwatoyama (also called Shiroyama or Himurogatake) – A conical mountain believed to be a sacred site since ancient times. It is a forbidden area, said to be a site where deities descended. Near the summit, there are believed to be remains of rock rituals. The site is designated as part of Kyoto Prefecture's historical natural environment conservation area.<ref>[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=581&r=d60d0f61d551f1d3a5d7fa9c3cf5075d 元伊勢天岩戸神社] (Kyoto Tourism Guide).</ref> == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical13.html Kyoto's 200 Natural Selections: Moto-Ise Three Shrines] (Kyoto Prefecture Homepage) {{Japanese mythology|qq=}} {{shinto shrines|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)|version=102337841|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:天岩戸神社 (福知山市)]]
